intro: Margaritopsis pallidinervia, also known as Pali-toki or White-veined Margaritopsis, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, and is used to treat various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Margaritopsis pallidinervia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include tea infusions, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to promote respiratory health and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu.
